summary: path-extend performs import-time remote code execution via jsonkeeper.com fetch + eval
details: On every require('path-extend'), the package's path.js invokes loadStringData(), which base64-decodes a hardcoded string to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/P0CND, fetches the JSON response, and passes its `content` field directly to eval(). jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous, mutable paste host — the attacker can change the executed payload at any time without republishing the package. A second loader (loadTokenData, https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/F0Z6M) is staged in the file. The package name and description impersonate Node.js's built-in `path` module, and path.js ships a verbatim copy of the Joyent path source as cover, with the remote-eval dropper as the only behavioral addition. Anyone who installs and requires this package grants the operator of those pastes arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
